notes: v1.14.8 — living globe STAGE 1 of 3: ambient cloud layer (builds on the v1.14.6/.7 lit ocean sphere). Fills the blank ocean with drifting soft cloud systems so empty faces read as a living world; CITIES UNTOUCHED. Architecture: clouds are soft point-puffs appended to the SAME static WebGL buffer as the city lights, so they rotate with the globe for free and reuse the whole render path. New mechanic: each cloud point carries a NEGATIVE aPhase sentinel (normal points always use Math.random()*6.283 >= 0); a small shader branch reads it. EDIT 1 (4 GLSL sub-edits across both shaders): added a varying float vCloud (VS + FS); in the VS, isCloud = step(aPhase,-0.5) is 1 for clouds (aPhase<=-0.5) and 0 for every existing point, clouds get a slow drift (twC = 0.80 + 0.06*sin(uTime*0.20+cph)) instead of twinkle and never flare (flare *= 1-isCloud), vAlpha uses mix(tw,twC,isCloud), and vCloud is passed through; in the FS the falloff softens for clouds (glow = exp(-r*r*mix(5.0,2.2,vCloud))). Every existing point (cities/coastlines/arcs, aPhase>=0) is byte-for-byte unchanged. EDIT 2: a self-contained IIFE before staticArr assembly generates ~1,591 cloud points via banded value-noise FBM (ITCZ tropics band + mid-lat storm tracks, not a uniform haze), R_CLOUD=1.012 (just above borders 1.004), large soft puffs (csz 6-18), cool blue-white additive color, each pushed in the standard 8-float vertex format with the negative-phase sentinel. Static buffer = built once = zero per-frame cost. Stage 2 (Pacific hurricane spiral) and Stage 3 (lightning) follow. Real visual feature -> quad bump v1.14.7->v1.14.8 + cache bump. Verified: node --check 3/3 inline scripts + sw.js, block-0 CSS braces 2736/2736 (no CSS change — all edits are JS incl. the GLSL string literals), CRLF preserved (0 lone LF), four stamps lockstep v1.14.8, sw.js cache-bumped, all cloud sentinel phases negative (cities unaffected), git diff surgical. GLSL VS+FS compiled + linked clean in a headless WebGL context (SwiftShader). Adversarially verified (4-lens workflow). NOT fully runtime-tested on the iPhone GPU — the ONE real residual risk is GLSL compiling on-device (compile() throws on failure -> blank globe); load on device and check the console if the globe goes blank. John's device watch: (1) soft cloud systems drift across the empty ocean face, cities exactly as before; (2) density knob = the -0.30 gate (raise to -0.45 for fewer, or +=3 step -> +=4) or lower toward -0.20 if too sparse; (3) brightness knob = cb base 0.30 + the 0.74/0.82/1.00 multipliers if clouds wash the SPECTRUM land gradient; (4) night-side glow — clouds are lit uniformly (shader doesn't know the sun); Stage 1.5 can tint by sun-facing if it reads wrong; (5) softness = raise csz or push the FS mix second number 2.2 down. WARNING: standing repo-only fixes — v1.13.8 (#boot full-bleed) and v1.14.4 (storage-full recovery) still lack a counterpart in the external/web-Claude build base; re-apply on the next import or they drop.
